key_themes: Weak 2026 guidance with expected net sales decline of 1.5-5.5% and adjusted EPS $2.00-2.30 amid soft OTC/consumer health markets
Q4/FY2025 earnings miss on adjusted EPS, massive goodwill impairment driving large reported loss, and ongoing Infant Formula headwinds
Aggressive cost restructuring including ~7% global workforce reduction targeting $80-100M annual savings; 2026 viewed as transitional year
M&A/takeover speculation (e.g., Stada rumors) providing short-term stock lifts alongside high ~9.8-10% dividend yield at depressed prices
Analyst mixed views (Buy/Reduce consensus split) with price targets $17-19 implying 45-65% upside but recent downward revisions
summary: Investor and media sentiment toward PRGO is negative in the near term after a Q4 2025 adjusted EPS miss ($0.77 vs. ~$0.80 expected), a massive $1.3B goodwill impairment, soft organic sales trends in OTC and Infant Formula, and conservative 2026 guidance calling for overall sales declines and lower CORE EPS. Management is countering with major cost actions (7% headcount cut, $80-100M savings program), divestitures, share gains in store brands, and a sharpened focus on core self-care, describing 2026 as a transitional year with H2 improvement and 2027 acceleration expected. The stock hit lows near $11 in March but saw relief rallies on M&A rumors; it currently yields nearly 10% and trades well below analyst targets of ~$17-19, creating a value/dividend debate amid limited but mostly cautious social/investor chatter.[[1]](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/stocks/articles/perrigo-prgo-positioned-growth-amid-211522278.html)[[2]](https://www.marketbeat.com/instant-alerts/perrigo-nyseprgo-reaches-new-1-year-low-whats-next-2026-03-20/)[[3]](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/perrigo-prgo-down-18-9-051044703.html)[[4]](https://investor.perrigo.com/2026-02-26-Perrigo-Reports-Fourth-Quarter-and-Fiscal-Year-2025-Financial-Results-From-Continuing-Operations)
sources: Perrigo official Q4/FY2025 earnings release and investor presentations (Feb 2026), 15+ recent news articles and analyses from Yahoo Finance, MarketBeat, Simply Wall St, Trefis (Feb-Apr 2026), ~25 low-engagement X posts and mentions of $PRGO or Perrigo since Feb 2026 (limited organic investor discussion volume), Analyst ratings and target updates (3-6 firms; targets lowered post-earnings but still reflect long-term value)
